hero.title: Worlds Worth Mastering.
hero.body: We make premium online worlds where players build the economy, fight for status, lose real stakes, and create stories that last.

foundersPass.title: lifetime founder passes
foundersPass.body: Founders Pass are limited NFT passes created to support the critical early development of the Majori Games ecosystem. Only 300 will exist. Founder sale price is $1500 until June 1, 2026, then normal price is $2500. Holders receive a monthly token reward of 4.44 UJU and 4.44 Makgura coins.

blog.title: What's happening
blog.intro: Studio notes, development updates, and worldbuilding drops from Majori Games.

posts.title: Majori Games reveals Ujura and Makgura
posts.excerpt: Two original online worlds establish the foundation for Majori's long-term universe strategy.
posts.body: Majori Games is building a connected ecosystem of online worlds with Ujura and Makgura at the center. Ujura focuses on sci-fi sovereignty, player industry, and risk-driven space conflict, while Makgura explores brutal fantasy PvP, clans, raids, and honor-driven warfare.
posts.author: Majori Games
posts.seoTitle: Majori Games reveals Ujura and Makgura
posts.seoDescription: Two original online worlds establish the foundation for Majori's long-term universe strategy.

posts.title: A sandbox economy where risk creates the story
posts.excerpt: Mine resources, build ships, control routes, fight pirates, and lose what you bring into battle.
posts.body: Ujura is designed around connected systems where every trip matters. Resource hauling, ship production, piracy, security status, PvP, and market pressure all create stories that players can actually affect.
posts.author: Majori Games
posts.imageStyle: radial-gradient(circle at 35% 22%, rgba(88,231,255,.68), transparent 19%), radial-gradient(circle at 72% 45%, rgba(91,124,255,.42), transparent 26%), linear-gradient(135deg, #030914 0%, #071a38 44%, #180a30 100%)
posts.seoTitle: A sandbox economy where risk creates the story
posts.seoDescription: Mine resources, build ships, control routes, fight pirates, and lose what you bring into battle.

posts.title: A brutal fantasy world built around clans and honor
posts.excerpt: Warbands, raids, territory, crafted gear, and PvP encounters with real consequence.
posts.body: Makgura brings Majori into a harsh fantasy world where clans, warbands, crafted gear, raids, territory, and player reputation shape the long-term conflict.
posts.author: Majori Games
posts.imageStyle: radial-gradient(circle at 50% 10%, rgba(240,179,91,.62), transparent 22%), radial-gradient(circle at 18% 65%, rgba(193,72,35,.30), transparent 28%), linear-gradient(135deg, #1a0d09 0%, #321b0f 45%, #070606 100%)
posts.seoTitle: A brutal fantasy world built around clans and honor
posts.seoDescription: Warbands, raids, territory, crafted gear, and PvP encounters with real consequence.

hero.title: A player-owned tactical space MMO where ships, planets, blueprints, and guild wars all connect.
hero.body: Explore a player-owned galaxy of high-sec missions, low-sec piracy, null-sec warfare, pirate faction NPCs, combat sites, contested planets, Veil raid systems, and a player-driven economy built around production, destruction, ownership, governance, ship loss, dropped loot, and the one-of-one Starcore that turns the universe into a warzone.

alpha.title: Enter the first gate.
alpha.body: Get early alpha access for concept art, faction reveals, ship drops, dev logs, blueprint launches, seed round updates, and pre-alpha play windows.

blog.title: Ujura updates from the frontier.
blog.intro: Development notes, lore transmissions, economy updates, and sale announcements for Ujura.

posts.title: The Starcore turns the universe into a warzone
posts.excerpt: Only one Starcore exists at a time, creating a global PvP flashpoint for null-sec alliances.
posts.body: The Ujura Starcore is a one-of-one war objective. It appears as a dangerous null-sec combat site, must be deployed at a star, and then becomes a global conflict zone where players fight for timed rewards and control.
posts.author: Ujura Team
posts.imageStyle: radial-gradient(circle at center, rgba(79,163,183,.34), transparent 34%), radial-gradient(circle at 78% 22%, rgba(141,219,236,.22), transparent 20%), linear-gradient(135deg,#020611,#0b1f33)
posts.seoTitle: The Starcore turns the universe into a warzone
posts.seoDescription: Only one Starcore exists at a time, creating a global PvP flashpoint for null-sec alliances.

posts.title: Blueprint ownership is production, not instant power
posts.excerpt: Master Original Blueprints grant production rights while ships still require resources, time, and risk.
posts.body: Blueprints in Ujura are meant to control production rights, not spawn finished power. Owners generate limited-use Blueprint Copies over time, and players still need resources, industry, logistics, and risk to produce ships.
posts.author: Ujura Team
posts.seoTitle: Blueprint ownership is production, not instant power
posts.seoDescription: Master Original Blueprints grant production rights while ships still require resources, time, and risk.

posts.title: High-sec, low-sec, and null-sec shape player risk
posts.excerpt: Security status defines where players can trade safely, pirate for profit, or fight for territory.
posts.body: High-sec supports safer progression, low-sec opens the door to piracy and richer rewards, and null-sec becomes the lawless player-controlled layer where territory, resources, and Starcore control create conflict.
posts.author: Ujura Team
posts.seoTitle: High-sec, low-sec, and null-sec shape player risk
posts.seoDescription: Security status defines where players can trade safely, pirate for profit, or fight for territory.

hero.title: Makgura
hero.subtitle: Level. Fight. Conquer.
hero.body: A persistent, player-driven war MMORPG where players level from 1-60, fight for Rome, the Barbarian Horde, or Egypt, become a Gladiator in Colosseum PvP, capture cities, control territory, and compete over dynamic world resources.

alpha.title: A persistent player-driven war MMORPG.
alpha.body: Players level, fight, lose gear, become Gladiators in Colosseum PvP, control territory, govern cities, compete over dynamic resources, and reshape a constantly shifting ancient world.

blog.title: Makgura dispatches from the front.
blog.intro: Development updates, worldbuilding notes, faction reveals, and economy design for Makgura.

posts.title: Professions and crafting power the Makgura war economy
posts.excerpt: Gathering, refining, crafting, building, trading, and guild supply give Makgura players economic roles beyond combat.
posts.seoDescription: Makgura professions include gathering, refining, crafting, building, trading, housing support, market supply, and guild war logistics.
posts.body:
Makgura professions are designed to make the world feel alive outside of pure combat. Players should be able to build a reputation as gatherers, refiners, crafters, builders, traders, outfitters, and guild suppliers.

Gathering professions pull value out of the world: ore, stone, timber, hides, herbs, rare resources, and dangerous warzone materials. The best materials should often require travel, risk, protection, or control of contested territory.

Refining professions turn raw materials into useful components. Ore becomes bars, hides become leather, timber becomes planks, and rare resources become the inputs needed for stronger gear, house upgrades, siege supplies, and market goods.

Crafting professions create the things players actually need: weapons, armor, tools, potions, repair kits, furniture, house upgrades, outpost materials, siege parts, and replacement gear after PvP losses.

The housing and land plot economy gives professions another purpose. Player houses can create demand for building materials, furniture, storage upgrades, decorations, repairs, and prestige items crafted by other players.

Guild warfare also needs supply chains. Sieges, raids, gold vein fights, and territory wars consume food, potions, gear, repairs, mounts, building parts, and replacement kits. That means a great crafter can matter to a warband as much as a great fighter.

The goal is a player-driven economy where combat creates demand, professions create supply, and city markets decide who gets rich.
posts.author: Makgura Team
posts.imageStyle: radial-gradient(circle at 22% 30%, rgba(214,168,90,.42), transparent 22%), radial-gradient(circle at 78% 28%, rgba(63,125,78,.28), transparent 24%), radial-gradient(circle at 52% 78%, rgba(184,50,42,.24), transparent 24%), linear-gradient(135deg,#12120d,#2c2414 48%,#070706)

posts.title: Makgura NFTs: founder cities and home plot supply
posts.excerpt: Makgura NFTs include 3 Founder Capital City NFTs and 600 home plot NFTs across Rome, Egypt, and the Barbarian capital with fixed SOL pricing.
posts.seoTitle: Makgura Founder City and Home Plot NFT Supply
posts.seoDescription: Makgura NFTs include Rome at 100 SOL with 300 plots, Egypt at 50 SOL with 200 plots, Barbarians at 25 SOL with 100 plots, and land plots from 0.15 SOL.
posts.body:
Makgura NFTs are similar to Ujura because they represent scarce ownership, but the structure is different. Makgura is built around ancient-world real estate, capital cities, faction identity, and player housing.

The top layer is 3 Founder Capital City NFTs. There is one Founder Capital City NFT for each race or faction capital: Rome, Egypt, and the Barbarian Horde.

Rome is the biggest capital city. The Rome Founder Capital City NFT is priced at 100 SOL. Rome has 300 home plot NFTs: 150 small plots, 105 medium plots, and 45 large plots.

Egypt is the middle-size capital city. The Egypt Founder Capital City NFT is priced at 50 SOL. Egypt has 200 home plot NFTs: 100 small plots, 70 medium plots, and 30 large plots.

The Barbarian capital is the smallest capital city. The Barbarian Founder Capital City NFT is priced at 25 SOL. Barbarians have 100 home plot NFTs: 50 small plots, 35 medium plots, and 15 large plots.

Across all capitals there are 600 home plot NFTs: 300 small, 210 medium, and 90 large. Small plots cost 0.15 SOL, medium plots cost 0.5 SOL, and large plots cost 1 SOL.

These plots work like real estate for player houses that players will be able to build in the future. The design goal is ownership and prestige, not pay-to-win. Founder city NFTs and land plots do not sell weapons, stats, or direct combat strength.
posts.author: Makgura Team
posts.imageStyle: radial-gradient(circle at 24% 28%, rgba(184,50,42,.40), transparent 22%), radial-gradient(circle at 78% 24%, rgba(45,128,197,.28), transparent 24%), radial-gradient(circle at 48% 76%, rgba(214,168,90,.38), transparent 28%), linear-gradient(135deg,#130d09,#2b1c10 48%,#070606)

posts.title: Makgura Coin (MKG) powers competitive rewards
posts.excerpt: MKG is Makgura's premium reward token, earned through Gold Vein control and Top Gladiator PvP with 22-minute emissions and 2-year halvings.
posts.seoDescription: MKG is Makgura's premium reward token with an 8M max supply, Gold Vein and Top Gladiator rewards, 22-minute emissions, and 2-year halvings.
posts.body:
MAKGURA (MKG) is the premium ecosystem token for the Makgura MMORPG. It is separate from normal in-game currency and is earned through competitive gameplay systems rather than bought power.

Max supply is fixed at 8,000,000 MKG. Distribution is planned as 60% player rewards (4,800,000 MKG), 10% public sale (800,000 MKG), 5% Founders Pass Holders allocation (400,000 MKG), 10% seed round (800,000 MKG), 10% team and development (800,000 MKG), and 5% treasury (400,000 MKG).

MKG uses simple Bitcoin-style scarcity: rewards emit roughly every 22 minutes and halve globally every 2 years. The goal is a long 20-25 year emission timeline with strong early incentives and a long tail of competitive rewards.

There are two primary earning paths. Gold Vein Control is the main guild-scale path, starting at 40 MKG per interval for groups that control, mine, and defend contested resource zones. Top Gladiator is the solo and small-scale path, starting at 10 MKG per interval for the highest-ranked PvP Gladiator.

Together, those systems start at 50 MKG per reward interval, matching the clean total-emission structure used across the Majori Games ecosystem while splitting Makgura rewards between territory warfare and Colosseum PvP excellence.

Utility is designed around premium convenience and prestige: cosmetics, visual upgrades, governance votes, global bank storage expansion, a small capped XP boost around 5%, and market fee reductions for active traders and crafters.

MKG does not provide direct combat power. It should reward skill, control, competition, and long-term commitment without turning Makgura into pay-to-win.
posts.author: Makgura Team
posts.imageStyle: radial-gradient(circle at 24% 30%, rgba(214,168,90,.56), transparent 22%), radial-gradient(circle at 76% 22%, rgba(184,50,42,.30), transparent 24%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 78%, rgba(45,128,197,.24), transparent 26%), linear-gradient(135deg,#17130d,#34200e 48%,#070606)

posts.title: Risk, territory, and recovery define Makgura
posts.excerpt: Makgura connects leveling, faction war, Colosseum Gladiator PvP, city control, loot risk, banks, housing, and world events into one ancient war MMORPG loop.
posts.seoTitle: Risk, territory, and recovery define Makgura
posts.seoDescription: Makgura connects leveling, faction war, Colosseum Gladiator PvP, city control, loot risk, banks, housing, and world events into one ancient war MMORPG loop.
posts.body: Makgura is built around a simple promise: the world should remember what players do. Leveling, faction choice, Gladiator ranks in Colosseum PvP, death risk, city control, resource conflict, safe storage, and recovery systems all connect so war has weight without permanently trapping players after a loss.
posts.author: Makgura Team
posts.imageStyle: radial-gradient(circle at 50% 10%, rgba(240,179,91,.62), transparent 22%), radial-gradient(circle at 18% 65%, rgba(193,72,35,.30), transparent 28%), linear-gradient(135deg, #1a0d09 0%, #321b0f 45%, #070606 100%)

posts.title: Rome, Barbarians, and Egypt shape the war map
posts.excerpt: Three playable powers create different routes into formation combat, raiding pressure, and trade-driven control.
posts.seoTitle: Rome, Barbarians, and Egypt shape the war map
posts.seoDescription: Three playable powers create different routes into formation combat, raiding pressure, and trade-driven control.
posts.body: Rome, the Barbarian Horde, and Egypt are built to feel different at every layer: combat identity, economy, territory pressure, logistics, and political control.
posts.author: Makgura Team
posts.imageStyle: radial-gradient(circle at 22% 32%, rgba(184,50,42,.34), transparent 22%), radial-gradient(circle at 76% 28%, rgba(45,128,197,.26), transparent 24%), linear-gradient(135deg,#12110d,#2b1c11)
